Hi Everyone -
I thought I would contribute to the collective knowledge here with my journey busting using Rivea Corymbosa Seeds, AKA "RC Seeds", AKA Ololiuqui. I will keep a log of it all in the format I've shown below. First I will give some quick background. (To skip to just the busting log, scroll to the bottom).
I'm a 35 year old male, first started getting CH when I was sixteen, in 2001. Cycles are generally ~7 weeks, and have been getting further and further apart the older I get, with ~3 years between cycles. When I was a teen I did the pharma route, finding mixed results with Prednisone taper and Inderal. But once those stopped working, a neurologist with a bad attitude started trying other drugs on me, and I started reading about the experiences of other CHers on clusterheadaches.com and here on clusterbusters, I said "no thank you" to the pharma route.
2003 - 2010
Sloppily experimenting with busting, different vitamins, prednisone/Inderal etc. I was 18-25 during this time, and you know how people are at that age! I had mixed results with mushrooms, and also had very mixed results with the high! One of the most enjoyable nights in my entire life was on mushrooms, and it stopped the CH dead in its tracks. I also had some of the most intensely anxious times in my life, and it didn't stop the CH. I attempted RC Seeds during this time, but back then the recommended starting dose was very low. I think I started with 8 seeds! So, I found no impact from using them.
2010 - 2020
Started the Vitamin D3 regimen. The D3 brought the severity of the pain during attacks WAY down. I've never been able to fully control a cycle using just the D3 regimen, but it has helped tremendously. During this time I also started using oxygen - *properly*! I was prescribed oxygen as a teenager, but was using the wrong mask and didn't have enough air flow, so I concluded that it didn't work for me. This was a total game-changer, obviously. I also started realizing that when I eat, I get attacks. I started talking to Batch a lot during this time. We talked about the body's PH and how keeping the body more alkaline could help. So I started drinking more lemon water, making tweaks like that, that I think helped a little bit. I tried 5-MEO-DALT for two cycles during this time as well. The results were mixed and I did not like the idea of weighing out these tiny, tiny increments of a random chemical I purchased in a sketchy way on the internet and ingesting it.
Latest Cycle - Started Nov. 2020
This cycle has been different for a number of reasons: 1) It came faster than expected. Typically the pain free time between my cycles increases by 3 months each cycle. It is crazy how accurately it has followed it my entire life! 3 months between my first cycle and my second, then six months, then 9 months, etc. etc. I was all the way up to three years between cycles, and then this latest one came after 2.5 years. Hellow to you all,
Since reading here on this forum about your successful stories and all the information that came with was very helpful to me, I want to return the favor and tell you mine.
I am 37 years old, live in germany and was first hit by the beast in my early twentys. After erratic outbursts in the beginning year, it settled at a 2 year cycle always starting in late autumn or winter, lasting 8 weeks with 3-4 attacks per 24h. (reading a while an this board, I now consider myself pretty lucky)
Every cycle i would go to a different doktor or neurologist and was sent home with various migraine pills. When I mentioned CH as a possibility, I was shut down by the dokters, because that couldnt be because did not show any facial paralysis… wich I actually dont, even at the worst „banging-my-head-against-something-hard-and-cold-pain“. So I went on popping chewed up Sumatriptan-Pills, untill the dokters wouldnt perscribe any more.
In 2022 I finally found a neurologist who`s first guess was CH. In his words: „What else should it be!?“ At that appointment my last cycle was over for about 2 months, so we agreed to get the O2 at the next cycle and maybe try cortisone infusions… the first idiea i liked, the second one not so much. So I startet searching the internet and found this forum. Thank you for all the hope you gave me.
Due to experiences in my younger years, I decided to try MM at the next cycle. And here is how it went so far.
The cycle startet at 28th December 2023. My cycle started very slowly with only 2 hits every 2 days in the first week.
First bust 06th January:
1,5g powderd MM in chocolate… turns out it works like lemon tekk, so it was a bit more intense then I planned. While coming down I got immediatly hit with a really bad slapback lastig 3h and 2h later again same as hard for 2h. The following night was quiet, but untill the next bust i had every night 2-3 attacks. None at daytime, only mild shadows.
Second bust 12th January:
1 g in chocolate. Had bad hadaches (not CH, but from all the sleep deprevation) while busting, felt terrible and the slapbacks came on like the fist time. Again i got hit the following days every night 2-3 times. At that point my spirits startet to leave me and I was questioning the whole process. Again reading all the similar experiences here kept me going.
Third bust 17th January:
1,25g same chocolate… That day was from the beginning out oft he ordinary. Had an strong but clear and short hit in the night and then shortly after getting up. That one was less painfull but would not go away untill noon. Every time i thought it would fade, it ramped up again. In the evening just before I took the MM i felt it coming on again, but it was stopped by the MM.
Coming down I felt the slapback slowly starting to burn in my face, but then fading away… that happend twice or more in the next hour and suddenly I had the strong feeling, that there wont be any more attacks. Like my body knows now how to keep it from getting out

21 year Episodic. Clusters used to happen every 9 months, lasted 1 month with 1 or two attack / day lasting 1 hour each. The cycle would be like clockwork, almost to the day. Back then I was treating with maxalt wafers only, and running cold water on my head. Eventually was prescribed amitriptyline, which unsurprisingly didn't do anything. Sometimes my cycle would
end
with a day-long attack. Different from a regular cluster headache, but, still on the cluster side. Really weird. Anyway, I would often end up in the ER with these ending attacks, but, they were always bad news / good news. Bad because I knew they would last at least 24 hours; and were brutal (not as bad as a CH attack, but, pretty unbearable due to the length of the attack and no sleep). Good because I knew the cycle would end after that one weird one. Eventually saw my first neurologist in 2012 after a trip to the ER (10 years after my CHs started). I've had all of the required brain scans, etc. and got the official diagnosis. Neurologist recommended maxalt wafers, home oxygen, prednisone taper at the start of a cycle, and verapamil for the duration of the cycle and for 6 weeks after it clears. Family doctor wouldn't prescribe me any of those things except for Maxalt. Said everything else was too risky.
Found a new Family Doctor 2 years later (2014). Got me hooked up with another Neurologist, and got the same treatment plan recommended. Got all of my scripts filled, etc.
Over the years, my cycles spread out longer (longer remission), but, more attacks while in cycle. One cycle, I didn't realize the risks of too many triptans (was taking 2, 3, sometimes 4 per day that cycle). Ended up getting really bad rebound headaches. Cycle lasted much longer, and I ended up getting upwards of 8 attacks per day. After that cycle, and realizing what had happened; I've tried to avoid the triptans whenever possible. I rely on home o2, and only use a maxalt nasal spray if I'm out and don't have access to o2 or any other options.
Started the D3 regimen in 2019, and have been taking all of the cofactors and everything daily since then.
For the last few cycles, my treatment plan has been:
1) 13 day prednisone taper at the onset of a cycle; 60mg 2 days, 50mg 3 days, 40mg 3 days, 30mg 2 days, 20mg 2 days, 10mg 1 day
2) Verapamil - 120mg x 3 / day
3) Home o2 - 25lpm through Cluster o2 mask (coupled with the odd sip of really cold ice water through a straw)
4) Rizatriptan Nasal spray only for emergencies (5mg)
5) Still doing the D3 regimen
6) Have tried adding melatonin in before bed (10mg)
7) Energy drinks to try to help clear shadows
8) Ice-pack on the head for shadows, and sometimes during attacks (other times I can't stand it)
9) No alcohol
The prednisone taper has killed 2 cycles right at the start. One cycle it didn't completely kill it (the cycle above with the triptan overuse); and I ended up doing a 2nd prednisone taper, which did end it
CURRENT CYCLE - started JUNE 17th

Hi there,
10 years ago I discovered this website and made a post here after experiencing pain abortion via psilocybe semilanceata use. Back then this board helped me understand so much better how to manage my CH cycles and attacks than any doctor ever did. So just for that, thank you!
As a funny story introduction, I have to tell you how I discovered the use of psychedelics for CH; After being diagnosed by a dental surgeon, I was talking about it to a friend, which told me "CH? like in Dr House episode with the mushrooms?" - this is what originally brought me to this website
Now without going into every single detail of what happened to me in the last ten years, I wanted to give a brief update on my journey, as I realize that the knowledge built on this site is for a big part generated by anecdotal information, here's my humble contribution:
Ten years ago I ended a cycle successfully for the first time using mushrooms. I was amazed and spent the next months reading everything I could as why this is happening. And while I dont have a real answer, my vulgar understanding is that CH are like a brain that had his road map messed with and mushrooms reset it to factory settings
During that period I was trying to abort individual attacks with originally coffee, mushrooms in very small dose under my gums and tongue, and breathing exercises. However I quickly replaced coffee with lemon juice as vitamin C seems to fast track psilocybin.
Anyways, after that cycle, I prevented successfully many cycles for many years using psilocybe semilanceata that I religiously pick myself every year. Meanwhile I have tried to get prescription for oxygen for years (in the fear that an attack would eventually show up) but always unsuccessfully.
However, about 3 years ago a cycle started again, still not sure as why, I was traveling a lot back then and messed up my sleep cycles regularly, this could be the reason, I was also drinking alcohol more than usual, i'm still not sure what exactly happened but it did.
So, I went to the doctor, reluctantly I have to say, because I still have bitter memories of what doctors made me go through prior to me discovering mushrooms for CH. Anyways, the doctor discovered that I had high blood pressure, and while he wasn't really acknowledged about cluster headaches he wanted to treat my blood pressure, which he said would likely reduce the intensity of the CH attacks too. And so he gave me isoptin (calcium blocker) and an alpha blocker, in low dosage.
This did indeed greatly reduced the intensity of the attacks and fixed my blood pressure. He also prescribed me an abortive nasal spray, Imigran (sumatriptan), which I never took because I wanted to break the cycle with mushrooms anyways and i'm very sceptical by now about migraines medecines for CH. I then managed to break that cycle by doing the mushrooms protocol, took 0.5g then 5 days, 1g and 5 more days and it stopped.
I went on with my life, I became a father even! 2 years went on, dosing

Hey,
I’m 21 year old male, 6’4/230lbs. I have been having episodes of cluster headaches lasting around 2 months for the past couple of years.
First time it happened (around 4 years ago), I went to several doctors and I was misdiagnosed with having a high blood pressure and I was prescribed daily medication to decrease it. I knew that the doctor planned to keep me on it for a very long time but I took it for a few months and I just stopped it. I did not plan to take it for my rest of my life. Surprisingly, at that time those medications decreased the intensity of my headaches and eventually it stopped.
The next year, I moved to study abroad in Montreal, Canada. At around December/January, I started having headaches all over again. I usually do dental work on my teeth and I thought that it might be caused from one of my tooth. After weeks fighting the headache and going to dentists, I resolved to the conclusion that I had to remove my wisdom tooth and eventually the headaches stopped. (I was prescribed lots of painkillers I remember a Codeine/paracetamol that I took daily along with sleep aids)
The year after, I was in my home country and I started getting the same problem all over again, I checked on my vision, I went to every doctor possible and at the end, I was referred to a neurologist after at least 3-4 weeks of daily headaches. That was the day I understood what is CH. I was prescribed with the regular routine including Prednisone. And the intensity of the headache kept decreasing till it eventually stopped.
I spent the following cycle with the same routine, however I did not want to take prednisone. Both my parent’s families had diabetes, including all the grandparents.
—-
3-4 weeks ago, my cycle started but I spent the first 10 days with very mild headaches that I could easily ignore and they usually go in 30-40 mins. I thought that I could just keep waiting and my whole cycle will stay at this intensity and indeed I did not take any single medication and kept waiting.
Right after the first 10 days, I had a really extreme headache, my body was extremely sweating, I was in a terrible amount of pain and It lasted at least 3 hours. Once that happened, I went online and ordered 5g of dried Vitamin M in capsules of 0.5g. Since that time my headaches intensity changed from almost no pain to 7-10/10 pain. Every day I would wake up, I will get a headache in exactly 1-2 hours and I would have to sleep and take painkillers and waste at least 3-4 to recover and start studying for my midterms. I insisted on not taking any triptans or prednisone although I has them right them right in front of me.
4 days later, (daily 1x headache 7-9 pain) I took my first Vitamin M dose of 1g right after I woke up (empty stomach). it skipped my morning headache but I was hit with another headache later at night. (I assumed it was a slap back)
I hoped my cycle would just stop or at least decrease in intensity but nothing happened. I started continuing my daily headaches

I sent this letter to two leading physicians, let s hope they can help
My 56 year old  husband has been suffering with cluster migraines  on and off  for about 10 - 12 years. Every 2 -3 years in the fall he gets a cluster, Oxygen is truly the only method that helps, and that is even hard to get. Insurance won't cover it for use in migraines and I can only get small tanks at $ 22.00 per tank, which lasts for about two to three headaches.
He has seen a neurologist who has prescribed  a steroid dose pack  with no relief,  imitrex which might shorten the duration, but we aren't sure, I read that too much imitrex  may  cause rebound effects with more severe headaches which I think was happening to him.
He has no psychiatric or concerning medical history except for  mild HTN.  He had numerous CT's and MRI's in the early years as well, all which were negative for serious disease.
I was able to secure some " magic mushroom  chocolate"  and with the guidance of the
clusterbusters.com
website we gave it a try and it worked like "magic."
We also  stayed off Imitrex for 5 days before dosing.
He took two "chocolate squares"  ( not sure of the dose of psilocybin in each, but this is what "our friend" usually eats recreationally)   on day one and he  had only one mild headache in the early morning.
so I had him take another 1 chocolate square on day 2, and no headaches for 4 days,
then another minor headache on day 4 , so he took 1 chocolate square last night....   No headache.
I think we will now go with  a 1/2 chocolate square  every 4 days.
We need more research on this wonderful drug.  It works, end of story,  As an RN for 38 years I have never seen anyone suffer so much and gain such relief from psilocybin.   He was becoming depressed, severely depressed and the physical toll during the headaches left him exhausted as well. He was at the end of his rope but now has hope.     He couldn't leave the house in fear of a headache, was afraid to drive, etc.   It is such a terrible condition that few people and physicians understand.
Psilocybin might not be the drug of choice for some patients, but there are other medications that  do not work for some patients as well.  Every drug is contraindicated in one way or another. Psilocybin and  depression was in the news again today., they said 5 % suicidal rate, Well antidepressants we currently prescribe have suicidal risk too.    Cluster headaches alone can cause suicide.
So my main reason for writing .....
Where do we go from here, how do we help these patients?
This is now a major goal of mine.   This information and medication need to be available to these patients.  No one should suffer with these headaches when there is a treatment that works.
I have seen some trials that you were both involved in ,   but what has become of them?   I / we/ other patients  need to get this drug in a legal manner. I am sure you two highly educated physicians can do something.
Please help these patients suffering with cluster migra

Hi all. I hope that it's ok to start a new thread. Sorry if it should be posted elsewhere.
A number of years ago, I unfortunately had recurring episodes of some awful cluster headaches. I’d previously described them on a now-deleted blog, and I’m posting here in case this is in any way useful to other sufferers going forward.
I’d first like to stress that:
I am not a doctor or healthcare professional.
Every human body is different.
We each react to supplements in very different ways.
What works for one sufferer may not work for another.
I have no affiliation, previous employment, or investment in Holland & Barrett.
As of December 2025, I’m a 45 year-old white male in good physical/mental health.
My first experience of cluster headaches was at the age of 20 (year 2000), at a time when we were starting to take advantage of what the WWW could offer us, but without the massive amount of data, research and shared experiences that we have in 2025.
Those first episodes hit me hard in the summer of 2020, and my GP at the time told me that they must be migraines, and that I should just take ibuprofen. I got a second opinion, and when the doctor in question said that the episodes sounded more like cluster headaches it felt like I was finally being heard and understood.
The regularity of the cluster headaches was both a curse and a blessing. They’d typically last between 10 and 20 minutes each whilst I was at work, and at almost the same time every day. The intensity of them, and the way in which I went from feeling nothing to agony was frightening. The regularity did at least mean that I could prepare. I tried espresso shots, throwing back a can of Red Bull, and/or running up multiple flights of stairs to varying degrees of success. After a few months of suffering, they’d go again until the next year.
From memory, I believe I had a couple of years without any episodes between 2005 and 2007. They came back hard in the summer of 2007 lasting 15 to 20 minutes at a time, and sometimes twice a day. In desperation I emailed Peter Goadsby (Professor of Neurology at King's College London) for advice. He said that he’d be willing to see me if my GP referred me. Around the same time I’d read on a CH forum that some sufferers had found different levels of success in taking supplements of taurine (found in Red Bull), calcium, magnesium and kudzu. In desperation I started taking the lot. I did ask Prof. Goadsby in that initial email if there were safe dosage limits that I should adhere to, but he didn’t acknowledge the question and I rightly/wrongly took that as a green light to carry on. I went to my local Holland & Barrett store, and bought the following supplement bottles:
500mg - taurine
Combined 500mg calcium/250mg magnesium
500mg of kudzu
I took these together daily.

st

Hello Everyone,
(*This turned into a book as I was writing it as I really allowed my passion to flow. Thank you to anyone willing to take the time to read it!*)
I am the mother of a 34 year old son who was diagnosed with CH when he was about 20. He wasn’t living with me at the time and when he spoke to me about his headaches he called them migraines so I only became aware of the actual diagnosis of Cluster Headaches and what that truly means recently. Since then I have been researching everything I can find on CH in my attempt to help him. This site has been invaluable for all of the information it contains as well as for everyone’s heartfelt sharing of their own experiences with CH. I am so appreciative!
While reading through the forums the other evening I had a major AHA moment as I started connecting the dots with CH and my son’s past medical history. As children he and his 2 years older sister constantly had strep throat and ear infections and were continuously on amoxicillin. Their younger brother somehow managed to avoid getting sick in a similar fashion. My husband died when my children were 4, 7 and 9 years old and losing the father they adored obviously impacted each of my children deeply, as it did me as well. 3 months after his death both of my 2 older kids underwent tonsillectomies since the strep infections were constant and amoxicillin was no longer keeping the infections at bay.
My daughter went on to become much healthier after the surgery, my son did not. While he stopped getting the strep throats that had plagued him for so long, he started getting constant stomach aches. Within a year he was sick so much of the time that he had missed over 1/3 of the school year. Our family practice doctors were at a loss to explain what was going on or diagnose him so referred him to a pediatric gastroenterologist who eventually diagnosed him with Crohn’s disease, a form of IBD (Inflammatory Bowel Disease) after tissue biopsy showed severe inflammation of parts of his bowel. The problem was that nothing Western Medicine had to offer him helped or alleviated his pain. Even prednisone didn’t seem to make much difference, all while he blew up like a balloon and suffered all the side effects that go along with steroids.

I had been working as a registered nurse for years so I was deeply steeped in allopathic medicine and hesitant to try something that wasn’t proven or necessarily approved by the medical community. Fortunately our family practice doctors were wonderful and encouraged me to take my son to see her. Hello everyone,
TL:DR
I may have discovered the cause of cluster headaches and have a working treatment plan, when previously very little worked for me. I went from fully chronic to them being almost completely gone. I've intentionally brought them back a few times, and then re-suppressed them to make sure it wasn't just luck.
These things have a cumulative effect, so while no one thing works well, in tandem there should be significant improvement. If you are desperate for relief, please give this a try:
Try clomiphene citrate, even if you've tried it before
Stay hydrated, drink lots of water
Listen to music in the mornings, especially music that gives you chills
Take 5-HTP in the afternoons
Try to destress / relax some - if an attack is triggered, try to remain calm and focus on breathing
Even if you've tried it before, start the Vitamin D regimen with all the cofactors asap. With everything, be very careful there's little to no soy in it. Some Vit. K gels are mostly soy. On sunny days, you may want to take more magnesium than usual, as your body is producing more Vit. D.
Lightly exercise as you can or go for a walk outdoors.
Avoid alcohol & sugar (including chocolate)
Avoid epinephrine (if you need dental work, share this with the dentist and ask for something without epi)
Try to minimize any opioid use (including kratom) if you have any for breakthrough pain. The only exception being Tramadol, as it can be helpful.
After being on the clomiphene citrate for a week or so, start tapering kudzu if you take it, as this can interfere with the clomiphene.
Be especially cautious of these things if you have a storm front approaching, as only one additional trigger will likely bring the beast back full strength.

Hey folks. After reading through a ton of posts on here and reconciling the various routines we seem to instantly start searching for, I noticed that this is a safe space for us. That sounds corny but it's true. I am 44 years old. Had my first encounter with the beast in May 2025. It lasted around 8 weeks. The best way for me to use words(if there are any) about how the pain of it hit me is "absolute, like gravity" or "it felt like an event horizon from a collapsing blackhole but in my head" and really messed me up. Im not even sure im recovered from the PTSD yet but I'm getting better with it. It took something from me and I'm even sure what but from everything I've been reading in these forums, it takes something from all of us. Maybe it's dignity it took? Because as I found out in the following months after it ended. It's going to find it's way back to me and I'm trying not to focus too much on that. So I will tell my story in hopes of inspiring us to keep pushing forward to better understanding of this because from everything ive searched and found, it is literally the CH community who seems to grasp this as good but usually better than any research abstract I've come across and I'm not saying that with any ego or pride. The neuroscience community is pretty open in saying that they don't fully understand CH. Immunology has some good research that connects to CH indirectly but speaking of egos, those fields seem to not communicate other than traditional cross-domain topics. The issue i keep coming back to is exactly what I just said. CH is a non-linear condition that, in my experience, won't be solved in a linear way. We need neuroscience, Immunology, psychiatry, and cardiovascular to start thinking outside the box and until they do, we got each other.

I am new here and wanted to share my experience with CH.  This site has truly changed my life and I want to return the favor for anyone that comes along.
I am 40 and have had migraines all of my life.  It runs in my family.  Large doses of caffeine and/or sumatriptan would get rid of these quickly and it didn't happen often.  No biggie - 5 time a year on average.
However, 5 years ago, I started experiencing a type of pain I didn't know even existed.  I have been to the E.R., had many MRI's, visits with headache specialists, neurologists, chiropractors, and nut jobs.  At no point did anyone know what was going on with me.  I was told it was just a migraine.  No sir!! I know what a migraine is.  Those are bad but this...this is worse than anything I can imagine.  This type of pain made me cry in front of my kids...sigh..wish that hadn't happened!  Made me want to physically hurt anyone who spoke to me.  It wasn't until this year that I found this site and wow!  Things have changed.
Year 1
- Started late summer.
- Pain levels of 8-10 ever night at 7pm almost to the dot.
- Could sleep.
- Terrible "shadow" for the entire day.  Repeat cycle.
- Went to doctor after doctor.  Sumatriptan helped reduce most pain 2 or 3 points down.
- During one attack I noticed how stuffy my nose was and that I couldn't breathe.  I was desperate.  Found an old bottle of Afrin and almost emptied the bottle in my nose.  Attack gone in 15 minutes.
- 7 months into it I went to an ENT.  Said I needed surgery.  I did.  Doctor aggressively trimmed down my turbinates. 2 weeks later - all attacks gone.
Year 2
- nada.  Nothing.  Life was awesome.
Year 3
- Year 1 repeated itself but on the other side.  Went back to ENT, had another surgery.  Same results.
Year 4
- nada.  Nothing.  Life was again normal.  Had some attacks that seemed random but mostly triggered by alcohol.  It took me a while to realize that was a trigger.  Saw neuro who said I had CH but he wasn't sure that was even a real thing.  Gave me Sumatriptan.  Whatever.  Attacks were on and off throughout the year and it was bearable.
Year 5 (current)
- started having small attacks after drinking with friends.  I immediately knew what was going down.
- by Mid-August I was having 5 attacks/day. (post wakeup, 3ish, 7ish, 2am waking from sleep.  Pain levels of 6,7,10,8 respectively.  Each one lasting 2-3 hours.  I am not sure life could be worse at the moment.
- by end of August I was taking 5 times the recommended dose of Sumatriptan.  It was enough to keep me sane but I used 30 days worth in 1 week.  Doc refused to refill.

Hi everyone. Was hoping to get some words of wisdom. Feeling exhausted and like I’m losing this battle. Little history, I was diagnosed back in 2004-ish. My very first cycle I was given the prednisone taper and I’d never touch it again even in desperation from how sh*tty that stuff made me feel. Other than that I’ve stayed completely away from pharmaceuticals. I consider myself extremely lucky to have had a relative that already
knew the correlation between CH & psychedelics when I was diagnosed! MM has always been my go to. I’ve never had a cycle last longer than 6 weeks with busting MM on the every 5 day mark. I utilize abortives- Red Bull/coffee/5 hour energy, O2 (welding), hot baths cold showers, ice packs when I can stand it etc etc etc!!! Sometimes I even throw in that sinus plumber pepper nose spray that seems to divert pain for a few seconds into a screaming nose ache…Without any maintenance busting, I was surfing a wonderful 3 year PF wave. Then last October 26th i woke at 1:38 am with the dreaded reminder the beast was coming. Got my welding tanks loaded up the next morning to have them filled but didn’t need to use them for another couple weeks, closer to Thanksgiving. The beginning of January was beyond rough. I was having heavy daytime shadows
at work, hiding my little O2 20 tank in my car and having full on attacks around 6 pm every day on top of the nightime after falling asleep..for about a week. I thought for sure this was the cycle ramping up to come to an end. But nope this bitch will not go away.
Fast forward to now, I procured a fresh batch of MM. these are by far my least fav batch I’ve ever had in all my years. I just don’t like the way they make me feel.

1/26 up within every 1 1/2 hours of falling asleep, O2 & Red Bull.
I’ve developed a nasty nasty cold and I’m terrified to take any type of cold meds in fear of aggravating the monster even more. Hi all - Quick update.
First, admins, is there a way to start a new topic on this that is appropriately titled?  This has turned out to be all about Emgality and I’d hate for it to be lost if it can be helpful to somebody.
As stated above, I got a second course of DHE infusions on December 1-3.  I actually found a private infusion center covered by my insurance (called Paragon Infusions, which is located in a number of states) that will do the 3 day DHE course.  The doc prescribed toradol, magnesium, reglan and DHE.  That keeps me there for almost 3 hours.  I declined everything but the DHE and reglan and can get in and out in just shy of an hour, which, if you can do it early enough in the morning, can get you to work by 10.  Not bad.
I took the 300 mg of Emgality on December 5.  First, the shadows and other sensations that I was having over the course of the day in November effectively eliminated during December.  That notwithstanding, I was still having breakthrough hits, but only at night, and only every 3-6 days during December and the first week of January. Also, the intensity was way, way down, and sometimes the hits were painless (wake up, eyes closed, could feel my face sweating and mind racing, usually fall back asleep, light shadowing the next day).  I am now 5 weeks post second dose, and the cycle seems to be winding down (now that I’ve said that I’ll probably have an attack tonight…..).  I haven’t had a drink since October, but generally things up there feel relatively normalized.
The bad news is the Emgality has made me severely constipated.  It doesn’t touch Verapamil’s constipation, but it’s still pretty awful.  Emgality’s half life is really long, so I don’t really know how long the constipation is going to last.  It does seem to be letting up some, and I’m hopeful that the discomfort will be more or less disposed of by the end of the month.  We’ll see.
My neuro wants me to stay on Emgality or some CGRP blocker full time.  We’re trying to figure out what dose I can tolerate all the time without the side effects (I’ll put up with a month or two or even three of severe constipation and discomfort, but not ALWAYS).  The plan is to try the 120 mg migraine dose and see if I can live with that.  We hope - without any evidence - that perhaps blocking enough CGRP in me full time will stop the cascading reactions that start the cycles to begin with.
The reality check, here, is that I’ve tried this before and it failed miserably.  In 2020, I felt the cycle ramping up a little in July so did 300 mg of Emgality in July, August and September.  The cycle broke through anyway and, by the beginning of October, I was in a hell of a lot of pain.  That cycle took 4 prednisone tapers and 9 months of verapamil to break.  I have theories about why it was so bad (tons of prednisone, tons of work-related stress, all turning into tons of personal stress).  What I think really made the difference this time were (1) the DHE

Hi to all.. a quick disclaimer: THIS IS LONG and I apologize. I've considered posting for years, but I'm so very much not inclined to "put myself out there" - maybe a bit of a technophobe.. but i've never chatted, instant messaged, facebooked, etc.Â  I have looked at the well known clusterheadache sites for years, found cluster busters a few years back,Â  and finally decided it's time to share.Â  I think one of the most frustrating things for me as a cluster headache sufferer is the fact that so few people have ever even heard of them, and every time i try to explain to people why i'm clutching my eye, or "am on the wagon" or have oxygen cylinders all over my house, i get the feeling that people just think i need to go get my little tin foil hat and put it on.. if you know what i mean....Â  I want to say to the "community" here that it is very inspiring the way everyone supports one another, and over the years just reading the posts has been so helpful to make me feel like i'm not alone. Others who understand how much this disease can rob your joy.Â  Â  So, thanks to all that have gone before me. I'm glad I found you and that folks were willing to put in the work to create this resource.
Background on me -Â  I'm a male 41 who first began having clusters around 19 or 20 yrs of age. Episodic sufferer now for last 15 years. Usually have one cycle a year, lasting 2-3 months.Â  They started randomly. Would get individual attacks - kip 3-6 with no real pattern. I wonder now if what kept the beast at bay for those first couple of years was the fact that on a few occasions in college (literally 3-4 times) i did small recreational doses of shrooms.Â  I was never a big fan of the idea of "seeing something that wasn't there" so i was very leary.. err.. paranoid of the whole hallucinogen thing - just wasn't my "thing".Â  I liked that i could try shrooms in small doses but when i tried them I always did enough to "feel" it but nothing too intense.Â  When i first read about busting, i was happy to read that you can have success with smaller doses than what is considered "recreational".Â  Â So, back to history..Â  I was around 25 when i had my first real cycle.Â  After about a week of 3 to 5 hits a day of what i used to call "my eye headaches" I finally knuckled under and headed for the family doc... convinced of my upcoming brain scan and diagnosis of brain tumor, aneurism, etc.Â  Doc listened to my symptoms and casually said, 'oh, you have cluster headaches'. (no scans ordered) i was relieved to find out the "what", but it would be several years before i found any real relief.Â  My doc prescribed prednisone to "break the cycle" but never really worked for me.. Each year, I would wait until the cycle became too painful to deal withÂ  and then get my prednisone, would take it, be fine while on it, then the beast would come back just like before.Â  For years i could set my watch by my attacks (between Sept - Dec) usually lasting 10-12 weeks. Hi forum, very glad to find this website!
After reading the various stories, I feel like many were like my own situation. So here is my story...very long and not especially extraordinary, but I thought to include all details in case it helps any other newbie someday (e.g. hey that is just like me). So sorry for the length of post.
I started experiencing clusters when I was 18yr in my first yr university, in spring (March-April) several times a day lasting for 4-6weeks. Unbelievable pain (always right side, started with shadows as I now know them to be called, then jaw and neck pain, then spreading to eye socket, felt like a tapered wooden peg, the kind to kill a vampire with,  being hammered in to the eye socket and out through back of skull, very bad), I took OTC pain meds (tylenol overlap with ibuprofen) but did nothing. Saw family doc, thought it was allergies, sent me to dentist but nothing there, sent me to optometrist (since I mentioned eye pain) and got crappy very low prescription. By that time, the cluster episode was over.
Then I did not have another for a few years, then came back again with same pattern and same thing (tried OTC paid meds, saw family doc, blah blah, run-around until cluster over. Then forgot about until next one, again 3yrs later again in the spring, very painful and was done university and working outdoors on the road travelling doing environmental tests out in the field, so could not call in sick, suffered through for the episode of 4-6 weeks. (Note: for the theory of low testosterone and low vit D...I am not sure about, I mean I was outside in the sun almost always during this work for 15hrs per day, and I was in my early 20s working out in gym like crazy, really good libido, so think my vit D and testosterone was fine, but was getting crappy sleep so totally could buy the upset biological clock connection)
Same pattern with episodes every 2-3yrs typically in spring for 4-6weeks with max frequency/day in middle of cluster, just suffered through and probably almost OD'ing on OTC pain meds which did almost nothing.
Then in my mid 30's when I was on parental leave for my first child and was the only care provider at home, when a cluster epodes started. I knew I could not watch this baby with multiple attacks daily, so went to ER hoping they could figure out. I am 47 years old and I have been suffering almost daily, and typically 2 or 3 times a day, from headaches on the right side of my head for six and a half months now.  First I had my teeth checked, then got a new prescription in my glasses.  Neither helped... Most often they initiate at a point just above the temple but slightly closer to my eye and about 1/2 inch beneath my skull.  It is a sharp point source that radiates outward and it is almost always in the exact same location.  I was initially treating the headaches with Ibuprofen and was tricked into believing that it helped, because the headaches would only last 30 or 40 minutes and I was attributing the relief to the Ibuprofen.  I suspected that the Ibuprofen had stopped working so I moved on to Naproxen then started stacking Extra Strength Tylenol.  After about 2 months of constant suffering I ended up resorting to the ER, where I had CT scans both with and without contrast.  Both were negative.  I then found a new doctor and was treated with oral Prednisone 40mg which had no effect on the headache, but turned off my ability to control myself, culminating in smashing my guitar.  So 6 days into the Prednisone I messaged my doctor and he told me to stop the Prednisone.  Subsequently, we tried Sumatriptan Nasal Spray 20mg.  I did not get relief so I gave up on it after 2 attempts.  Later, I returned to the Sumatriptan, using 10 doses in 8 days only to realize that I was being tricked again in the same way as I described with the Ibuprofen.  I was then scheduled for an MRI which was also negative.
I had heard about psilocybin mushrooms as a treatment for cluster headaches and have tried several times.  The mushrooms always completely turn the headaches off for the duration, then significantly reduce the intensity, duration and frequency of the attacks for a few days.  Today my doctor finally prescribed oxygen as an abortive and Topamax as a preventative.  I am afraid to start taking the Topamax, as I was only just able to stop taking antidepressants (primarily Zoloft) 20 months ago, after having taken them for 20 years for severe depression.  I know that the Topamax will interfere with psilocybin as well, so I don't think I'm going to take it.  I have high hopes for oxygen therapy but it was a real hassle trying to explain how to write the prescription.  Then, the medical supply company that he gave the order to wanted to fix me up with an oxygen concentrator.  Fortunately, I refused and was able to go back into the office and request a different provider that could supply a type M tank, regulator and mask.  I am waiting on the insurance to approve the treatment....
I'm convinced that these are cluster headaches, but I am worried that my headaches are different from cluster, and seeking feedback regarding symptoms.  So about 80% of my attacks last about 30 to 40 minutes and I would describe my level of pain at around 6.  I think this number might be diminished by a high tolerance to pain, bu

Hi fellow sufferers,
Two months ago I was diagnosed with cluster headaches. I’m 29 years old. It started in mid-November. At first the attacks were “moderate” and always came at 10 a.m. After about a week, the intensity increased. The attacks became more severe and now came at 10 a.m. and 3 p.m., usually lasting 1–2 hours.
I felt it in my temple, my jaw, above my eye, below my eye, in my nose, at the back of my head, and on my scalp. It felt like someone was hammering a huge nail from top to bottom straight through my face. I could even feel it in my palate. I get goosebumps just thinking about it. I thought I was going to die or had a brain tumor or something similar. At first there were only the attacks, and afterwards I was pain-free again.
After 5–6 severe attacks, a one-sided constant pain above my eye set in, which lasted 24/7, and the attacks seemed to arise out of that. The pain first brought me to my family doctor, who couldn’t help and sent me to the hospital after three days with no improvement. At that point I was taking ibuprofen like candy with no effect. I still kept taking it because I thought: at some point it has to work.
At the hospital I described my symptoms to the neurologist: a blocked nose, a watery eye, and head and facial pain that I didn’t think a human being could have and survive. She immediately recognized it: cluster headaches. I was then treated with triptan injections. The attacks were stopped, but the constant headache remained.
I had never heard of this disease before and started reading about it while still in the hospital. I used to be a smoker and quit the same day. Later I was treated at a pain clinic that had experience with cluster headaches. I kept a pain diary and read more and more about it. I started supplementing D3K2. And I found out that bright light triggers the attacks. The intensity of the attacks became somewhat lower—until they finally disappeared after about two weeks. However, the constant headache remained and I began to develop very intense light sensitivity. From then on, a baseball cap and sunglasses were my best friends.
I remembered that I had something similar about three years ago, though not with this intensity of attacks. Back then they thought I had sinusitis and I thought my wisdom teeth were simply causing problems. It was already intense enough at the time that they did a CT scan and found nothing. But it wasn’t comparable to the attacks now, and back then I didn’t even realize they were “attacks.” But I do remember the same constant headache above the eye that lasted for weeks and then simply disappeared from one day to the next. That’s when it became clear to me that it had already been cluster back then, knocking for the first time.
To get rid of the constant headache and end the episode for good, the doctor at the pain clinic prescribed prednisolone. He said normally they would also add verapamil, but since the attacks were largely over, it wasn’t necessary (thank God). In t

She told me to take some OTC meds and was once given a migraine prescription med, which did nothing. After trying the tough it method during the episode early this year, I had finally reached my breaking point. The CH came for a 5th day in a row while I was at work. I told my boss that I couldnÂ’t work like this any longer and that I would be taking time off to pursue treatment of this condition. I walked out of work that day and immediately called my doctor.
Deep down I knew what the condition was but I was too afraid to admit it. I had done research a few years back and my symptoms aligned with the condition known as Cluster Headaches. I was in denial at the time and never told anyone what I thought it was. From what I read, there was no magic pill to cure it, so I did not want to admit to myself that I had an incurable condition. I simply tried to ignore it.
However, on that day I went to my doctorÂ’s office and decided it was time to confront the beast. I told my doctor they were not migraines but were Cluster Headaches. She said she didnÂ’t know much about it and then referred me to a neurologist. The neurologist said it sounded possible and after he left the room for 10 minutes he came back and confirmed it was CH. Finally, it was like a weight lifted off my shoulders. It was a day a dreaded but finally putting a name to the horrible condition gave me some sore of relief.

Overall, the side effects from all the drugs had me feeling like a zombie and I was still getting the attacks. After a month without any relief I went back to the Doc and told him about the side effects, and he simply gave me different abortive medication and upped the verapamil dosage. I have not posted on this site before, but have been skulking around it for years and I figure I may as well add our story because without so many of you (who don't even know it!) things would be so much different!
My husband is an episodic cluster sufferer.  Started in 1995 and came on hard and fast.  "Suicide headache" is about the only appropriate term for what he would suffer.  (And I loved the GP whom I tried to explain this to who suggested my husband might need to be checked into a facility if he was throwing around terms like suicide.)  My husband would get headaches every 6 or 12 or 18 months and the doctors didn't diagnose it for years.  It was a TERRIBLE time for him.
It took years before we got the cluster diagnosis and finally found Prednisone which ... kind of ... helped.  The steroid made him crazy.  Literally.  Aggressive, impatient, angry and mad which are polar opposites of his character.  Also, many times, the clusters would 'break through' when he weaned down to 10mg (after starting at 70 or 80!) and he would have to start the prednisone all over again.
10 years ago he found oxygen and that was a godsend.  Together with Imitrex and handfuls of whatever other pills were in the house, I could keep him vaguely able to handle the intense amount of pain that gripped him.  It was especially fun when our son was born and he was hit with the worst cluster ever (Spring 2003) and couldn't tolerate any noise at all.
We continued at this pace until 2008 when he was hit in March.  Prednisone allowed breakthroughs FOUR times and he was still having headaches in August.  The doctors (GP and Neuro) kept prescribing ever increasing doses of Prednisone - together with Topamax (migraines) and Verapamil (tension headaches) which had never done him any good.  One night when I was about at the end of my tether, I found this website - and it changed everything.
We did the cleansing, hopped on the Vitamin M trail, and never looked back!  Every three months kept them at bay the first year.  Every four months and nothing the second year!  Really, it was like a miracle!!  It has been hard for me not to stand on a soap box and shout this out to everyone I meet - but instead - we remain quietly at home, trying to keep it on the down low.  He never saw his neurologist after that because he didn't want to have this kind of thing in his medical file.
Enter Thanksgiving this year.  We had grown complacent and moved to a six month maintenance schedule for October.  We hit mid month and he realized he had a tiny bit of Vitamin M left.  He took it - but it had no effect.  I literally WATCHED the headaches come over the next few days.  His right eye will begin to droop just a bit and then grow more and more flat.  We only have one place to procure our vitamins and there was a major miscommunication.

She's in her early thirties, lives alone, has a successful but stressful career that's hard to keep up because of the CH. Hello,
I was diagnosed with cluster headaches last fall, and am almost out of my most recent 2 month cycle of severe headaches. As far as I can tell I'm chronic, because I get episodic (2-3 times a day, last 1-5 minutes, one small area of my head, my eye tears up, yada yada) cluster headaches during my "remission" off period. My bi yearly 8 week "on" cycle feels much more like i have a masochistic midget strapped to my back, tightening my head in a vice, and stabbing me repeatedly in the back of my neck. Fellow sufferers like yourselves at least, will know that this is not an exaggeration of the pain.
This is my first time trying to reach out to other people who suffer from this. I'm the only one I know, and as far as my doctor, and the neurologist, they had never treated a severely chronic case, something of which I understand is fairly rare? And the more I research it, the less I find out they know about it. It is frustrating explaining it to people, "headache" is a severe under exaggeration of the pain, and I'm not sure if it's a self defense mechanism at play but why is it always those closest to you that secretly hope that it is or may be psychosomatic?
Imitrex doesn't work, I can't afford any other treatments off insurance. I spent $350 to talk to a neurologist for 11 minutes before he smiled and told me to make a next appointment, without a prognosis, and giving me no information. My doctor treats lighter cluster cases, she diagnosed me, the only treatment she has to offer for me is literally handfuls of vicoden a week, and 6 week prednisone therapy. Of which by no surprise leaves me feeling raw, agitated, and gross after 2 months, with a parting gift of a bleeding ulcer, and i can only imagine what all that tylenol is doing to my liver.
I'm most likely going to have another 2 month period again in september, and I just can't do vicoden again (literally, my body is becoming too dependent upon it), so in lue of knowing this does not substitute for formal medical advise,  does anyone have a suggestions for alternative treatments, or just any advice or support?
Unless oxygen will abort my whole 8 week period, I can't rely on it seeing as when I'm in an "on" cycle, I get like 8-10 headaches a day, lasting almost seamlessly from 1-2 pm to 4-5 am in the morning, and I'm restaurant industry, so that means my whole work day, and I just can't picture myself lugging an oxygen tank to work.  And as I'm sure you people know, the pain is so debilitatingly severe, it ceases all daily functioning.
I'm a professional cook, and I read lidocaine drops eventually kill your sense of smell and taste, so that's a very last case scenario, as well as the capsasin sprays.  My doctor wanted to try a new antidepressant that supposedly helps with pain, but if high doses of vicoden barely takes care of it, I'm doubting an anti depressant will. Any advice anyone can give me would be helpful I'm very new to this.
And this brings me to my last question: does any one else

Hey all!
I have started the busting process and was hoping for some suggestions from some experienced busters about next steps.
Quick (Reader's Digest version...lol) background: I have been a cluster head for 23 years (I am 54) following a TBI, episodic turned chronic 645 days ago. I have had an average of 5 hits a day for these 645 days. I have been through 6 neuros due to retirements, moving away or insurance changes. Over the years, I have not found an effective abortive or preventative. Yes, I have basically tried everything except electrical stim. No, O2 does not work, yes I have a high flow regulator and recommended rebreather mask. Vit D regimen does not help, I am allergic to triptans and blood pressure meds cause issues as I have a low resting heart rate and lower (but healthy blood pressure). Prednisone had given some relief in the past during a taper, but does not work anymore. It also caused me to become diabetic. Was on insulin injections for more than a year, but just metformin now. Again, have tried many many other treatments. I have a strange body chemistry that causes me to suffer terrible side effects from basically every medicine, even caffeine. I don't drink or smoke. What I do know for sure is that sleep is a trigger and otherwise, my hits are by the clock precise. I have gone through it all as far as emotions go. This last 645 days is no exception. Dread when the "cycle" started followed by my usual can do attitude to the why me's for a couple months following the cycle not ending and averaging less than 4 non-consecutive hours of sleep a day. I am in can do mode right now, but as with all of us, it is a battle with a lot of ups and downs.
Current: no abortives or preventatives, I am just riding them out. I did complete the third round of botox 3 months ago... no help, just side effects. Neuro and I are talking electrical stim next.
Busting: I know cycles are tough to break once they take hold. Being chronic, I am always in cycle. I was able to get some gummies with penis envy from a reliable source. I am in a situation where I have only been able to bust 1x per week on Mondays due to several circumstances. I know it should be 5 days between and am working on making that happen. Speaking of this, does this mean bust on Monday, wait 5 days and bust again on Sunday?
Attempt 1: 1 gram, no effects, felt nothing and no ch help.
Attempt 2: 1.5 grams same as attempt 1.
Attempt 3: 2 grams. Mild trip, seemed like it was what is suggested. Missed my next late afternoon hit. Nighttime brought more hits, but lower intensity. The next day I had major shadows all day but missed the morning and afternoon hits. Nighttime returned to as before after about 3 days and my daytime hits were much lower intensity. Definitely helpful.
Attempt 4: 2 grams. Less of a trip, and made me sick. Rough night that night and shadows all day again but daytime hits were not as intense. Nighttime hits less intense, became more intense each day to next bust. Dayt

Hello, I’ve been getting cluster headaches for probably 20 years now.  I only get them maybe once (for 1-2 months) every two years usually when the seasons change.  I usually get about 3 headaches a day during this period.
I had a doctor I saw in Florida and the main treatments was prednisone, verapamil, and then an abortive like sumatriptan.  They also prescribed high flow oxygen.   They also prescribed anti-convulsants but I never tried it..  I guess I was scared of the side effects.  Usually by the time I  get all the meds and regimen going, my cluster cycle ends.
Fast forward to now, I now live in Japan where it’s harder to get a high flow oxygen due to limited resources.  So, I have some meds I saved (from 2 years ago)..  I started 50mg prednisone, 80mg Verapamil per day, and that keeps the pain down but I still end up using about 50mg of Sumatriptan per day.   I have the 20mg nasal spray for emergencies as well.
My current problem is that I’ve been on 50mg of prednisone per day for over a month now and I hate taking prednisone.  My skin is also breaking out and I’m not sure if it’s due to the prednisone doing something to my body or if the breakout (heat rash or something) is from taking super hot showers to help with the pain of the cluster headache?
When I anticipate an attack, I usually take 40mg Verapamil, and 30mg of prednisone, then I go to the 24 hour gym (very painful drive usually at 3am) and I either run for an hour or do the stair stepper (with deep breathing exercise's) for an hour or until the pain is manageable.   I usually end up jumping in the shower several times as well because the hot water helps to distract the pain I guess.  During the attack, It feels like there’s a fog in my head and focusing and waking up will help with the pain.
When an attack happens, I feel a nerve running down the left side of my head/neck and into my upper back is cramping.  I can feel this nerve run from my upper left side back into my neck and then up into the left side of my cranium and that’s where the intense throbbing pain is.
As most of you know, REM sleep can trigger these headaches so I often wake up out of a super deep sleep with a headache and an intense urge to run and get oxygen into my body.  I also try stretching the muscles in my neck but I’m not sure if that helps or aggravates the headache.  It feels like if I sleep on the left side of my head, my chances for waking up with a headache are increased?
I just now started reading about the Vitamin D3 regimen and I’ve started taking the Vitamin D3, but still have to purchase the rest of the regimen.   I really feel like I must start tapering off of the prednisone, and I need to stop the sumatriptan due to the rebound effect.
I’m flying back to the US this weekend for four days because I have some shrooms I want to take to see if that will stop my cycle.  I’m desperate to stop this a cycle because it’s debilitating.
I’m posting this just to see if anyone has any good suggestions.  Si

Hey everyone! I'm a fellow cluster head who stumbled upon this absolutely amazing resource recently after seeing a documentary on using psilocybin mushroom to treat CH. First and foremost THANK YOU for making this available whoever you are! This is an awesome support and treatment information resource I only wish I had found sooner.
Today was my first dose of MM as I'm attempting to bust a recently started cycle, I'm 5 wks in. I had pretty much gathered(please correct me if I'm wrong or give any advice you may have) that the dosing for the busting method is 2g once then again 5 days later, and again 5 days after the 2nd dose.
Just some backround info on myself and prior/current treatments & meds: 27 year old male w/ episodic clusters beginning at the age of 15. Cluster periods typically extend from July-November (sometimes long/shorter and begin or end earlier/later) but in a nutshell thats my "typical" pattern with a few surprises here n there. They ramp up over the course of 3 or so weeks to my average of 4-5/day mostly all extremely strong, very rarely do I get any that I wouldn't kill to get triptan relief from. I was misdiagnosed with migraines for the first 3-4 years. During the period of misdiag I was treated with topomax, depakote, opiates and botox to no avail and with major side effects. I got frustrated and sought a specialist and landed at a headache center in NY. From that point forward I've been on "conventional" treatment. Imitrex aborts a HA almost instantly 5-10 mins, 4or 6 mg inj both work the same. Zomig has been a go to when Im at home and can afford to suffer longer in peace i.e. not on the job. Once the cycle would start my doc would get me going on Verapamil 120mg instant release 1x in the morning 1x at lunch and 2x before bed(I don't think it helps much but I've always just done it) he's suggested higher doses but I get very lightheaded almost a vertigo feeling especially going from prone to standing, and didn't care to increase it. Prednisone has been my savior(wicked side effects but it worked) it would instantly stop all headaches from first dose till about 1-2wks after my last dose. I'd taper 60mg/day for 7 days then 50mg/day for 2 days, 40mg for 2 days and so on. This would often get repeated at least 1 other time and very rarely, only at my begging, a third time(doc said the prednisone is a nasty drug and wants to keep its use to a minimum). When they would come back I'd get an occipital nerve block - usually 3-4 during the course of the cycle. Sometimes they help sometimes not.
While in no way shape or form was I getting total relief, the cycles were "tolerable" and I put up with the nasty side effects of the meds. This time around the headaches started coming back before I even finished my prednisone taper. I found the busting method after searching like crazy for any new meds that might be out or anything that might help. This led me to the only immediately available option of MM. I haven't taken imitrex since bef

Hello,I am new to the site. I am very thankful to have found people who understand the situation I find myself in. My first experience with a headache that only affected one half of my head (left side)happened about Fifteen years ago. I was at a work function, enjoying cocktails with friends when I suddenly hag a really bad headache on the left side of my head. I left the function and went back to my hotel room and took an Alka-Seltzer Plus cold medicine and it went away in about a half an hour. I didn’t have another episode until about four years ago. It started again with cocktails, and blew up very quickly into a terrible left side headache with what felt like a #2 pencil in my left eye. It felt like my sinuses were really blocked. I went t my doctor and was treated for a sinus infection with Amoxicillin. Everything went back to normal, until about three years ago, when I felt like I had another sinus infection, same headache. More antibiotics, problem solved. This happened about once a year until three months ago. I had a cocktail and the headache was back with a vengeance, I also discovered that Viagra was a trigger. I saw a nurse at a Minute Clinic and she prescribed saline spray, Flonase, and Prednisone. It didn’t help and the headaches came every night without alcohol or Viagra. I saw my doctor and she prescribed Amoxicillin which had worked for a sinus infection about six months earlier. Ten days later, I still had the headaches every night. I went back to my doctor and she prescribed Prednisone Z pack. That didn’t help, but she said she thought I was probably having Cluster headaches. She referred me to an ENT and a neurologist. The ENT looked at my sinuses and said that I have a deep sinus infection. He suggested minor surgery to open up the passages because my medical history indicates that I have sinusitis. He suggested that I may be experiencing cluster headaches, and while the surgery won’t prevent the headaches, it will likely help my sinus problem. The same day I met with the neurologist, I told him my story, and he said that I just described a textbook case of cluster headaches. He said that they are not curable and not easily treated. He looked at the two CT scans that I had from the last six months that showed the sinus infections and is still convinced that I have CH. I continue to have the headaches every night and sometimes during the day. The neurologist said the headaches could last from four to six weeks and then disappear for a year or more. About six weeks ago after a visit to the ER one night for headache pain, l had no headaches the next night. They were gone for two days and then came back. I thought I was out of the woods , but no luck. Anyway I am scheduled for minor sinus surgery in two weeks. There seems to be no rhyme or reason to these headaches. My doctor gave me a prescription for ten mg Prednisone tablets in case I feel a bad one coming. I had not taken any until last week, when I had a really bad headache.

Hi to everybody!
First thanks to all of you and the forum. I appreciate very much your efforts in fighting the`Beast`...
I am a EC since about 20 Years, having my first Cluster Attack after a root extraction of a tooth. My Episodes have been reliably precise of one Attack late at night for 2 months, followed by a remission period of also 2 months. For about the firs 14 years. Then I camew across a Neurologist, who had treated me with Verapamil and Sumatriptan. The first shot of Sumatriptan was so amazing and wonderful. There was finally something, that worked on the Attack. Verapamil has aborted about 3 or four cycles. It had completely no effects on the following cycles except massive sideeffects.
From there, it has totally messed up my reliable pattern, and episodes got longer, (remission periods also).
During the last full year, i had not a remission period at all, except under Prednisone, starting with 100mg/day, reducing to 0 within 3 weeks.
as soon as the Prednisone is out of my body, the Pain starts again.
During this year, the frequency ranges between 2 and 8 Attacks/day. (that means also 2 to 8 Sumatriptan injections/day) So i´m now chronic.
I have been on Oxygen, Naratriptan, Prednisone, Lithium as well as numerous `unconventional` treatment attempts like Headachepowder obtained from a Maassai-Healer, inhaling burning Elephant-Dung, Spiritual Healer, electrified acupuncture needles, Regimes of Vitamines,
up to six cans of Red Bull/ day, Ocziptal-Nerve blockage, Gammacore and Tens-stimulation.
Marihuana doesn´t work either. It just makes the time in between the Attacks more pleasant....
2 years ago, i tried Sclerotia with no succes. But as I learned now, that it may not work while in Triptans, i wanted to give it another try under abstinence of the injektions. Then i kept using Sumatriptan injections very frequently.
3 weeks ago, i started with aprox. 3g of fresh Sclerotia (as 10g is a recommended recreational dose).
2nd dose after 3 days, 3rd dose after 5 days and a fourth after 5 days, but this time 1.6g of dried Psilocybe cubensis.
each time the psychoactive experience was very similar although not entirely pleasant, as i believe, that this amount gets me in between the two worlds, as many users experience.
anyhow, after the first dose, i had an attack the next morning, but on Level 3-4 (usually attacks are ranging on 7-9 on my personal painscale of 0 to 10) and lasting only 10-15 minutes. No injections needed.
Some days no Attack at all, usually one relatively mild Attack. But they do get stronger and last longer every day, until i took the next dose.
The last dose i took the day before yesterday, no Attack until last night, pain level 1 for 6 minutes, and another one followed this morning of Level 4 for about 15 minutes
during the last 2 weeks i tried the SPUT- method on 3 Attacks, with relief of 2 of them.
So all in all I see a huge difference in frequency, but mostly severity of the Attacks. And I´m Injection-free since the first

Hello from Australia,
Not as familiar with the CH lingo and hopefully posting to the right area. But I do know the Beast well. Him and Me have had many discussions about his malign and evil presence. The pain has been something only a fellow sufferer could appreciate.
First started getting CH 35 yrs ago and effective treatment was non existent. It took 5 yrs to be diagnosed not by a Dr but by a nurse. In the beginning, attacks started off when I went to bed and after dealing with intense pain would go back to bed only to be woken some 25 min later with another one coming on. This would last all night. As I said treatment was non existent. The CH would commence at the turn of the seasons and last for up to 3 months (twice year) . Looking back I don't know how I managed the pain every night for so long. My attacks would last for 30-40 min each. I decide to log every one to see what triggers were occurring or some sort of pattern.
If I started to get headaches after drinking beer, (sometimes just a shot of liquor would do it) then I knew CH's were on their way. It filled me with a deep dread & a knowing of helplessness. Within 3-4 days they would be on me.
Have tried many treatments over the years, (panadol, Tramadol, some Peth, Oxycodone, Lithium, Verapamil etc etc) mostly to no avail. Our GP Dr's are not well versed in CH and to a large degree you are on your own. Medical knowledge is certainly not as mature as US and no clinics specialising in this area. Online communities sparse.
I have had the CH change sides twice. I also suffered migraines in between attacks with full aura.
Currently dealing with a bout that has gone for 29 days straight (4 attacks during day and 6/7 at night). The CH  are starting in the late afternoon, maybe this is what are called shadows and get progressively worse till eventually morphing into a full blown CH some 2-3 hrs later. These different to the night time ones which come on after going to bed. Some 25 min later after falling asleep I wake and CH goes ballistic within 5 min. Not a lot of time. Insomnia is a thing now and the CH have impacted me greatly. I feel my brain has been rewired due to CH and subsequent treatments. Not all of it positive.
Its very difficult if not impossible to get oxygen due to living in a nanny state where home use of oxygen is considered a safety hazard. Mostly I manage CH with starting a course of Prednisolone up to 60mg a day for 10 days. This seems futile when there is no treatment other than Immigran to fall back on.
When a CH starts I get very active doing very vigorous exercises, (this would give me 5-10 min grace) and then take an Immigran tablet, hoping it will cut to the chase before the pain goes ballistic. This works 2/3 out of 5 . I do have needles but I only use these when desperate or just before passing out. The needles cost $60 each and are not subsidised. Hi friends,
I'm not super active on here, but this community has been such a huge resource for me in so many ways.
I've been an episodic sufferer for the past 20 years, going into an attack cycle every 1-2 years for about 6 weeks. After going through a very negative medical experience with various doctors, specialists, brain scans, etc when I first started experiencing CH (where the final diagnosis ended up being migraines
), I largely avoided doctors until 2 years ago, when I was in my last cycle. I thought, "it's been 18 years, there must be new research/medicine to combat migraines," so I went to a neurologist. The neurologist ended up diagnosing me with Cluster Headaches, which opened up a whole new world to me - it introduced me to this wonderful forum and its amazing, empathetic community members. It was also then through the forum that I first learned about the busting technique, and so I tried it out - and it worked! However, that was towards the end of that cycle, so I can't be 100% certain the busting technique stopped the cycle, or if it just stopped on its own.
Fast forward to now, I am in another cycle - and it's a particularly brutal one. The night I got my first attack, I busted with 1g in tea form. I got an attack immediately afterwards. I continued to get really brutal attacks, averaging pain level 8, 9, or 10 out of 10 with awful nausea and vomiting, every night. As this is my first time busting at the onset of a cycle, I told myself this was normal and soldiered on.
I unfortunately used up the last of my MM with that 1st bust, so the 2nd bust (5 days later) was with an acid tab. Big mistake. I had the worst nausea and attack I've ever felt. It's really something else to be tripping during an attack. I continued to get brutal 8/9/10 level attacks every single day following the 2nd busting attempt.
I was able to procure some additional MM, so I ingested 1g for my 3rd busting attempting (5 days after the acid fiasco), and that actually seemed to have worked. Pre Diagnosis:
I’m 38 and had my first ever cycle of cluster headaches this year, starting the first week of March. Preceding the cycle, I experienced three overlapping stressful events that spanned several months. First, I suffer from Raynaud’s syndrome, which has never been all that bad, except for the past winter, when it became MUCH worse than years past (moderately stressful due to impact on quality of life). It prompted blood work to confirm that the Raynaud’s was not secondary to a different, underlying problem. The results came back indicating that my Rheumatoid Factor was high, which validated that my Raynaud’s was secondary, confirming that I had some sort of autoimmune disease (heavy stress). This prompted another round of blood work to find out which autoimmune I was “blessed” with. The kicker was that it took a full week for the blood work to come back, which was more than enough time for me to research all of the potential autoimmune diseases it
could
be (HEAVY stress). It turned out to be Rheumatoid Arthritis and Sjogren’s syndrome which was probably one of the better outcomes.
Nearly immediately after the diagnosis, my wife and I signed a contract on a new house and had to put ours up for sale (more stress).
Throughout all of the above, at work, there was continuous HEAVY stress, as we worked on an account critical to our company.
Diagnosis:
Fortunately, my wife is a Nurse Practitioner and has a wealth of knowledge and access to subject matter experts, which ultimately led to my quick diagnosis & treatment. My treatment was a high-dose steroid taper (prednisone), starting at 60mg. The steroids stopped my attacks, but holy crap – my body does not handle high dose of steroids very well at all, but that’s a story for another time. I got in to see a headache specialist and was put on verapamil 320mg and got several abortive tools prescribed as well, O2, sumatriptan auto-injectors & zomig nasal spray.
Post Diagnosis:
I have not had a full attack since receiving the steroids. I still get shadows, but they really don’t register more than a 1-2 on the pain scale and they seem to bounce all over my head, never in the same spot. I’ve gone multiple days without any shadows. Yet I've had one memorable “shadow” that hit me in mid-conversation with a friend, going to from 0 to 6 instantly, lasting for about 15 seconds and then there was an odd residual feeling after the pain subsided, like it was ready to come back at any second, but it didn’t and that odd feeling went away too.
This is where I’m sort of in limbo. I don’t have a lot of “cluster-history” to pull from, so I’m not sure what exactly is happening now, which is where my questions come in, assuming I’m episodic:
Does the instant “0-6” event sound like a shadow or something different?
Do shadows persist outside of a cycle?
Do shadows bounce all over the head?
How do you know you’re out of cycle?
Is it possible that I’m still in cycle, but the verapamil is working?

My Neurologist just told me about this website.  I am a long time episodic CH sufferer.  It’s been going on for 23 years.  My episodes usually last about ten weeks and I usually have a remission period of about a year and a half.  This has been an interesting episode.   Took Emgality immediately after the first initial CH attack.  Then started a high dose prednisone.  In the higher dose of the prednisone it does a great job of completely preventing the CH attacks.  Once it tapers down to about 30mgs the CH attacks come on and come on hard.  I used to take sumatriptan tablets, but they are very slow to get relief as you need to wait for it to be ingested into your blood stream.  I now use the sumatriptan auto injector.  It is amazing how quickly it gives relief from the CH pain.  Within 5-10 mins my pain is gone.  The only problem is you are not supposed to take more than 2 shots in a 24 hr period and I have 4 CH attacks  in a 24 hour period.  Also for the first time I tried occipital nerve block injections.  I have done  them once a week, 3 times now.  My experience has been that the day of and day after the injections it almost like it pisses off the Cluster and I have more intense CH attacks, but about 4 days later the frequency and severity has lessened.  Curious to hear from any CH sufferers who have done the occipital nerve block injections and what their experience has been with it.  My neurologist said that most patients get quick instant relief and it often will break the the cycle completely, not the case for me.   I also am aware that using lots of sumatriptan injections can cause the rebound headaches therefore extending the frequency and or duration of the CH attacks.  My neurologist prescribed me Oxygen for the first time which I have never done before and in doing so, the goal was to use the oxygen and only use the sumatriptan injections no more than twice a day.  Unfortunately I am having a hell of a time in trying to get it.  I live in Media PA, does anyone know of a medical supplying company that is easy to deal with in regarding getting the oxygen?  I am stuck in this limbo of them saying insurance needs more information from my provider and then my Dr saying no one has reached out to her about anything.  I read that someone uses tiger balm for pain relief.  I just started g that as well.  It doesn’t stop or prevent
